child_adolescent_core.full_prompt:
Produce a comprehensive discussion of this week's key findings in child and adolescent psychiatry. Cover each paper according to its own focus — methods, findings, clinical implications, and what a child/adolescent psychiatry resident should take from it. Generate the podcast entirely in Hebrew.

========================================================================
WHAT KIND OF EPISODE THIS IS (say right after the disclaimer, in Hebrew):
========================================================================
Right after the disclaimer, orient the listener to what this episode covers — naturally, in the hosts' own words, conveying this idea:
  "בפרק הזה נסקור את המאמרים שפורסמו השבוע בכתבי העת המרכזיים של פסיכיאטריית הילד והמתבגר."
Weave it into the opening; do not read it robotically or name an internal category.


========================================================================
HOW TO SPEAK (most important — read first):
========================================================================
Speak at a calm, unhurried pace. There is no need to rush or to cram — take the time each point needs. Every sentence must be COMPLETE and well-formed: never start a thought and jump to the next one, never cut a sentence off, never leave a fragment or a half-word hanging. It is far better to say less, fully, than more, in broken pieces.
This is a TWO-HOST Hebrew conversation. Keep it a real dialogue: the hosts genuinely alternate — one never delivers both sides or answers their own question. Keep each host's voice stable start to finish. Hebrew is gendered: each host addresses the other with the correct gender forms CONSISTENTLY (a male addressing a female says 'את אמרת'; a female addressing a male says 'אתה אמרת') — never flip mid-episode.

========================================================================
MANDATORY OPENING — say this FIRST, verbatim, calmly:
========================================================================
'הפודקאסט הבא נוצר באופן אוטומטי באמצעות בינה מלאכותית. התוכן עלול להכיל אי-דיוקים, פרשנויות שגויות או המצאות. אין להסתמך עליו לקבלת החלטות קליניות. חובה לבדוק כל פרט באופן עצמאי מול המקור המקורי.'

Then open with a short framing (1-2 min) anchored in a SPECIFIC paper or question from THIS WEEK's papers — a tension between two findings, or a clinically actionable question they raise. Do NOT explain what child psychiatry is (the audience is a resident), and do NOT reuse the same generic opening as previous weeks. Anchoring it in this week's papers keeps it fresh automatically.

========================================================================
FOR EVERY PAPER, state two things:
========================================================================
1. The journal, by its FULL name, not its abbreviation. The source lists each as 'Full Name (Abbrev)' — read the full name aloud (say 'Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry', not 'J Child Psychol Psychiatry').
2. The study type, taken verbatim from the 'סוג מחקר:' field in the source (מטה-אנליזה, RCT, מחקר עוקבה, וכו'). Do not guess. If it says the generic 'מאמר מחקרי', infer a more specific design from the abstract if you can. Study type matters — an RCT weighs differently than a case report.

========================================================================
COVERAGE, CONTINUITY, TONE:
========================================================================
Cover EVERY paper in the source — none skipped. Give each its own time (methods, findings with effect sizes, limitations, clinical implications); do not compress several papers into one sentence.
If the source ends with a 'משבוע שעבר' section, those papers were covered LAST week — do not re-summarize them. Use them only to draw a brief connection when a paper THIS week extends or contradicts them; otherwise ignore the section.
Keep the tone professional and measured. Avoid superlatives ('groundbreaking', 'revolutionary'); always name limitations and effect sizes. The gap between 'effective' and 'highly effective' matters.
